floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: saTan3-family replay/option menu frame handler (draw areas + cursor grid + smooth-scroll + pad input), built entirely from idioms already COMPLETED in this TU (S60C8 descriptor, fun

frontier.hypothesis: A full draft from the phase map -- descriptor struct + literal duplicate field assignments + plain /488 and /30 divisions + doubled-mask pad literals -- closes the bulk of 688 in one pass; this function is idiom-assembly, not discovery.
frontier.mechanism: All building blocks are byte-proven in this TU: S60C8 descriptor (func_800600C8), func_80073728/func_8007352C call framing, ot_Link chains; GCC 2.7.2 flow.c does no MEM dead-store elimination so the double-stores survive verbatim; expmed.c expand_divmod synthesizes the 0x4325C53F//488 and 0x88888889//30 sequences from plain division.
frontier.next_probe: Write the draft into the src/text1b.c stub, `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox func_800720FC --disable all`, classify residual by phase (the 5 ot_Link blocks give clean landmarks).

frontier.hypothesis: The [4,6] range gates compile to the observed `addiu -4; sltiu 3` from a plain `if (x >= 4 && x <= 6)` on the u16 globals (fold-const.c range collapse), NOT from hand-written `(u16)(x-4) < 3`.
frontier.mechanism: fold-const.c merges the && of two comparisons on the same operand into a single unsigned range check during genrtl; the operand is the lhu-loaded u16, so the subtract stays in SImode with sltiu.
frontier.next_probe: Draft with the natural && form first; if the compare shape diverges in the sandbox diff (branch sense or sltiu operand order), fall back to the explicit unsigned-subtract spelling and re-measure (rule ref: switch-vs-ifchain-branch-sense).

frontier.hypothesis: The cached menu base stays a STACK slot (sp+0x58, reloaded twice) rather than a callee-saved reg because its two uses are >600 insns apart with 18 intervening calls -- reproduced naturally by declaring it as a plain local assigned in the prologue region, NOT by re-deriving *(D_800A35A8->0x80) at each use.
frontier.mechanism: global.c spills low-refcount pseudos live across many calls to stack (allocno priority ~2 refs vs huge live range); re-deriving at each use would instead emit fresh gp-rel loads at both sites (different byte pattern -- lw gp_rel + lw 0x80 vs lw 0x58(sp)).
frontier.next_probe: In the draft declare `u8 *menuBase = *(u8**)(D_800A35A8->0x80-ish)` once before the first initDrawArea; verify the sandbox diff shows lw 0x58($sp) at both consumption sites; if GCC rematerializes instead, raise the local's liveness by using it in both lbu index expressions only (no other refs).
